title: proc_prune sometimes does invalid assignment promotions leading to multiply-driven wires
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Reduced from an nMigen testcase, run `proc` on:

```
module \top
   wire width 4 input 1 \a
   wire width 2 input 2 \b
   wire input 3 \clk
   wire width 4 output 4 \q
   wire input 5 \en
   wire width 4 \nq
   process \p
     assign \nq \a
     assign \nq [1:0] \b
     switch \en
       case 1'1
         assign \nq [3] 1'0
     end
     sync posedge \clk
       update \q \nq
   end
end
```

## Expected behavior

`nq[1:0]` connected to `b`, `nq[2]` connected to `a[2]`, `nq[3]` connected to a mux output, no other drivers on nq.

## Actual behavior

`nq` ends up with multiple drivers on low two bits, connected to both `b` and `a[1:0]`. The problem happens in `proc_prune` when it decides to promote the `nq[1:0] <- b` assignment into a connect, while leaving other assignments into the same bits.
code_blocks:
module \top
   wire width 4 input 1 \a
   wire width 2 input 2 \b
   wire input 3 \clk
   wire width 4 output 4 \q
   wire input 5 \en
   wire width 4 \nq
   process \p
     assign \nq \a
     assign \nq [1:0] \b
     switch \en
       case 1'1
         assign \nq [3] 1'0
     end
     sync posedge \clk
       update \q \nq
   end
end
